name: Double Nut Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease two cookie sheets with softened butter and dust with flour, shaking off the excess.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, ground almonds, sugar, eggs, rum, liqueur, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and baking powder. Beat until well blended.
Stir in the walnuts and whole almonds. The dough should be soft and a bit sticky, but it should hold its shape when picked up. If needed, add more flour.
With your hands, scoop up half of the dough; shape into a long cylinder about the length of a roll of paper towel. Place on one cookie sheet so that it does not touch the edge of the pan. Repeat with the second half of the dough.
Bake for 45 to 60 minutes, or until golden brown and firm. They will spread during baking.
Remove cookie sheets from the oven to a wire rack. Let stand for 15 to 20 minutes.
Carefully loosen the cylinders from the cookie sheets using metal spatulas. Let stand on a cutting board until almost at room temperature.
With a serrated bread knife, cut each cylinder into crosswise slices about 1-inch thick. Place sliced cookies cut side up back onto the cookie sheets.
Return the cookie sheets to the oven for about 10 to 15 minutes to let the cookies turn slightly golden and cool completely before storing.
